senses.definition: a series of numbers at the beginning of a phone number that specifies a region, town, etc.

examples.x: I accidentally dialed the wrong area code when trying to reach my friend in another city.
Before calling a different region, make sure you include the correct area code.
My phone automatically recognizes the area code and shows the city name.
She had to look up the area code for that part of the country before making the call.
If you’re calling from overseas, do not forget to add the international area code.

senses.definition: the pages in a phone book with the list of phone numbers of government departments

examples.x: I looked up the phone number for the local library in the blue pages of the directory.
The blue pages of the phone book were helpful when I needed to find the contact information for a government office.
I checked the blue pages to get the number for the nearest hospital.
If you're looking for a service provider, the blue pages in the phone book are a good place to start.
The blue pages list various organizations and businesses that offer the services I need.

senses.definition: the act of talking to someone on the phone or an attempt to reach someone through a phone

examples.x: During the call, he shared the good news.
I got a call from the school about my son's performance.
I received a call from an unknown number.
Please hold the call, I will be back in a moment.
She makes a call to her family every Sunday.

senses.definition: a phone call intended to return a call received previously
a person who is calling someone on the phone

examples.x: She recognized the caller's voice as soon as she answered.
The caller left a message after not getting an answer.
She asked the caller to repeat their name for confirmation.
He thanked the caller for providing helpful information.
The caller hung up quickly after realizing they had dialed the wrong number.

senses.definition: a system that shows the phone number of an incoming call

examples.x: She checked her caller ID to see who was calling.
He ignored the call when the caller ID showed an unknown number.
He set up caller ID on his phone for better identification.
She was pleased to see her friend’s name on the caller ID.

senses.definition: a phone service that enables one to know or answer an incoming call during another call

examples.x: I was talking to my friend when call waiting notified me of another incoming call.
She had to put me on hold because of call waiting, but then she came back to finish our conversation.
I ignored the call waiting beep because I did not want to interrupt my conversation with my mom.
The call waiting feature let me take my friend’s call while I was already chatting with my partner.

senses.definition: a phone service set up by a company or organization to provide its clients with information about the service they received or a product they purchased

examples.x: The careline was very helpful when I needed to cancel my subscription.
I called the careline to ask about the warranty on my new phone.
If you have any questions about the product, you can reach out to the careline.
I was able to get quick support from the careline when my order didn’t arrive on time.
The company offers a 24-hour careline to assist customers with any problems they encounter.

senses.definition: the practice of making an unexpected phone call or visiting someone in person in order to sell them goods or services

examples.x: Sales representatives use cold-calling to generate leads and expand their customer base.
Cold-calling requires effective communication skills and knowledge of the product or service being offered.
Some consumers find cold-calling intrusive and prefer businesses to use other marketing strategies.
Cold-calling scripts help sales teams stay focused and convey key selling points to prospects.
Regulations govern cold-calling practices to protect consumers from harassment and fraud.

senses.definition: a phone call that the person who receives it agrees to pay for, instead of the caller

examples.x: I received a collect call from the hospital asking me to confirm an appointment.
The operator asked if I wanted to accept the collect call from the prison.
When I was traveling abroad, I used a collect call to contact my parents.
The collect call from my friend was unexpected, but I accepted it because I hadn’t heard from her in a while.

senses.definition: a phone call in which three or more people can hear and speak with one another

examples.x: We scheduled a conference call to discuss the new project with the team in different offices.
We need to arrange a conference call with the client to finalize the details of the contract.
During the conference call, everyone shared their ideas for the marketing campaign.
The conference call had some technical issues, but we managed to get through the meeting.

senses.definition: a system for communicating music, pictures, and lengthy written messages between mobile phones

examples.x: I received an EMS from my friend that included a funny video clip.

senses.definition: a visit or phone call that is made as a gesture of politeness

examples.x: The customer service team made a courtesy call to ensure I was happy with my new phone.
He received a courtesy call from the hospital to remind him of his appointment.
The sales representative made a courtesy call to thank me for my business.
She received a courtesy call from the airline letting her know about a flight delay.
The hotel staff made a courtesy call to check if everything was to my satisfaction.

senses.definition: a phone service that provides advice, comfort, or information regarding specific problems

examples.x: She contacted the helpline to get advice on how to handle the emergency.
After losing my job, I called the helpline to learn about available resources for unemployment benefits.
I called the helpline for assistance with my Internet connection issues.

senses.definition: a direct phone line for emergency calls or calls between heads of governments

examples.x: Political campaigns use hotlines to encourage voter turnout and provide information on polling locations.
The IT department established a hotline for employees to report technical issues and request assistance.
Crisis hotlines operate 24/7 to ensure immediate assistance for those in need.
The suicide prevention hotline provides confidential support and counseling to individuals in crisis.

senses.definition: a phone connection using underground cables or wires on poles, rather than the satellite connection

examples.x: She still uses a landline for reliable communication at home.
He called his parents on their landline because cell service was poor.
They installed a new landline phone in the kitchen.
The landline provided a clear connection during the storm.

senses.definition: a telephone connection or service

examples.x: He dialed the number, and a voice answered on the other end of the line.
I need to make an important call, but the line is busy.
She placed the receiver back on the phone and hung up the line.

senses.definition: any telephone call made within a particular area and using the single switching center

examples.x: I made a local call to my friend just to check in.
He quickly made a local call to the office to confirm the meeting time.
Local calls are free on most mobile plans.
I only need to make a local call to reach my family, since they live in the same city.

senses.definition: a button that can be pushed to turn off the sound on a television, telephone, etc.
an enclosed place with a public phone that someone can pay to use

examples.x: She stepped into the phone booth to make a private call.
He found an old phone booth on the street and decided to use it.
They waited for their turn to use the phone booth at the train station.
The phone booth had a sign indicating the rates for calls.
She closed the door of the phone booth to avoid distractions.

senses.definition: the act of speaking to someone or trying to reach them on the phone

examples.x: I received a phone call from my friend just as I was about to leave the house.
The phone call lasted over an hour as we caught up on each other's lives.
She scheduled a phone call with her doctor to discuss her recent health concerns.
He was surprised by the phone call he got from an old colleague after many years.
During the meeting, she stepped out to take an important phone call regarding a job opportunity.

senses.definition: an electronic device that jams a radio or an electronic signal
a feature on a phone by which a phone number that is already dialed can be called, pressing a single button

examples.x: I have my mom's number set on speed dial so I can call her quickly whenever I need to.
You can save your doctor's number on speed dial for emergencies, making it much faster to reach them.
My phone automatically dials the taxi service number when I press the speed dial shortcut.
After programming his boss’s number into speed dial, he could contact her with just one button.

senses.definition: a tall pole or post that carries wires used for telephone lines

examples.x: A bird's nest was built at the top of the telephone pole.
The workers climbed the telephone pole to repair the wires.
A car accidentally hit the telephone pole, knocking it down.
A squirrel ran across the wires attached to the telephone pole.
The old telephone pole was replaced with a sturdier one.

senses.definition: an amount of money that is paid for a long-distance phone call
a phone number with a distinct three digit code that is free of charge for the calling party

examples.x: The company’s toll-free telephone number is available 24/7 for any inquiries or issues.
If you need assistance, just call our toll-free telephone number and a representative will help you.
You do not have to worry about any charges when you call the toll-free telephone number.
For technical support, you can call the toll-free telephone number listed on the website.

senses.definition: a system that allows callers to leave recorded messages for someone who is unable to answer their phone

examples.x: She checked her voicemail after missing a call from her boss.
The voicemail notification alerted him to an urgent message.
He forgot to check his voicemail and missed an important update.
She prefers voicemail over text messages for longer explanations.

senses.definition: a phone call that is made at a particular time to wake someone up, at their request, for example in a hotel

examples.x: She requested a wake-up call at 6 AM to ensure she wouldn't miss her early flight.
The hotel staff provided a friendly wake-up call to start his day.
He relied on the wake-up call service to make his morning meetings on time.
She was startled by the wake-up call but appreciated the prompt service.
They asked for a wake-up call to be well-prepared for their morning excursion.

senses.definition: the section of a phone book that gives the list of the names, addresses and phone numbers of individuals and businesses in an alphabetical order

examples.x: I looked up his number in the white pages to give him a call.
The white pages of the phone book had the contact details for all the local businesses.
He was able to locate the service provider’s number in the white pages.
I remember spending hours flipping through the white pages when I needed to contact people before the Internet became popular.

senses.definition: a section of the phone book with a list of the names, addresses and phone numbers of organizations and firms in an alphabetical order

examples.x: I looked in the yellow pages to find a nearby plumber.
The yellow pages had a section for every type of service imaginable.
He spent hours flipping through the yellow pages looking for a good electrician.
My dad still keeps a copy of the yellow pages in case we need it.

senses.definition: a phone communication in which conversations can be heard or recorded by others
the practice of selling products on a TV program or online
a mobile radio telephone that is designed to be used in a vehicle

examples.x: The car phone was a lifesaver when I needed to call for help on the highway.
I remember when my dad used to have a car phone in his old sedan.
The car phone didn’t work very well in remote areas, so we switched to using a mobile.
Back in the day, people would install a car phone to stay connected while driving.
My grandfather still talks about how impressive his car phone was when it first came out.

senses.definition: the part of a telephone where the handset or receiver can be put and be charged while not being used
the part of the phone held to the ear through which one can listen and speak

examples.x: She picked up the handset to answer the incoming call.
He adjusted the volume on the handset to hear better.
The cordless handset allowed her to move freely while talking.
She put the handset down after finishing the conversation.
He bought a wireless handset for convenience.

senses.definition: a cellular service that allows one to send and receive color pictures, sounds, etc. on their cell phone

examples.x: I sent her a picture of the sunset through MMS because it was too beautiful not to share.
The MMS feature on my phone lets me send both videos and photos to my friends easily.
He received an MMS with a funny video from his friend during lunch.
She quickly sent an MMS with the map to help me find her house.
I used MMS to share my vacation photos with family back home.

senses.definition: the central part of a phone system by which phone calls in a hotel, office, etc. are answered and put through

examples.x: The receptionist answered the phone and transferred the call through the switchboard.
She worked as an operator, managing the switchboard and directing calls to the right department.
We need to fix the switchboard because some calls are not being connected properly.

senses.definition: a telephone device with a screen and camera that can transmit video and audio signals

examples.x: With the videophone, I can have face-to-face meetings with colleagues from different cities.
He gave me a call on the videophone to show me his new house.
During the job interview, they asked if I could connect via videophone to discuss my qualifications.
I prefer using a videophone when I need to have a more personal conversation with friends.

senses.definition: a written message that one sends or receives using a mobile phone

examples.x: She sent him a quick text message to let him know she'd be late.
He received a text message from an unknown number.
The notification sound of an incoming text message caught her attention.
They communicated mainly through text messages throughout the day.
After the interview, she sent a text message to thank the hiring manager.

senses.definition: a cellular service of sending and receiving brief text messages on a mobile phone
a group of numbered buttons on a surface used for operating a TV, phone, computer, etc.

examples.x: The security system required a passcode to be entered on the keypad before granting access.
She dialed the phone number using the keypad on her mobile device.
The ATM machine had a touch-sensitive keypad for entering PIN numbers.
He pressed the buttons on the keypad to unlock the door to the office.
The remote control for the television had a numeric keypad for channel selection.

senses.definition: the part of a telephone, radio device, etc. to which the mouth is applied
the system through which different devices can be connected to each other over short distances wirelessly using radio waves

examples.x: The Bluetooth technology in my car allows me to connect my phone and stream music wirelessly while driving.
We used Bluetooth to transfer photos from our phones to the computer without needing any cables.
The new headphones come with Bluetooth connectivity, so you can listen to music without being tethered to your device.
Many modern smartwatches feature Bluetooth capability, enabling them to receive notifications from your smartphone.
The wireless speaker connects to my phone via Bluetooth, so I can play music from anywhere in the room.

senses.definition: a form of online communication which enables the users to communicate very quickly in real-time

examples.x: They prefer to communicate through instant messaging rather than email for quick responses.
The team uses instant messaging to coordinate tasks and share updates throughout the day.
She sent him an instant messaging app to discuss their weekend plans.
She received an urgent message from her colleague via instant messaging about the change in meeting time.
Instant messaging is ideal for quick updates and urgent matters.
